title: AI Companies Are Buying Tons of Old Books Because They're Free of AI Slop - Slashdot
excerpt: An anonymous reader quotes a report from 404 Media: As AI companies search for more training data to improve their models, one company is offering old, printed books as an ideal source because they are guaranteed to be free of the very AI slop AI companies are producing. "The world's best AI traini...
content_html:
An anonymous reader quotes a report from 404 Media: As AI companies search for more training data to improve their models, one company is offering old, printed books as an ideal source because they are guaranteed to be free of the very AI slop AI companies are producing. "The world's best AI training data is sitting on a shelf," ISBNdb, a company that produces what it claims is "the world's largest book database," and that offers high-volume book acquisition services for AI companies, says on its site. "Books represent curated, peer-reviewed, domain-specific human knowledge, structured in a way no web crawl can replicate. Dense, edited, authoritative."

In one article on its site, ISBNdb explains that printed books published before 2022 are ideal for AI training data because they don't include AI generated text. As the article correctly notes, much of the data that AI companies can scrape from the internet today is likely to include AI generated text, which could result in "model collapse," a process by which AI models that are trained on AI generated data results in worse models that are more prone to errors. The article also notes that book authors who object to their writing being scraped for training purposes can now easily poison AI models by producing writing designed to manipulate and sabotage the resulting AI models.

"Print books from the pre-LLM era are structurally guaranteed to be free of this contamination. That alone is a significant advantage [...] "Physical books published before this date [pre-2022] are structurally clean of modern poisoning tools." [...] ISBNdb advertises that it can keep the identity of AI companies secret. "Strict NDA [non-disclosure agreement] on every engagement," ISBNdb's site says. "Every project begins with a legally binding non-disclosure agreement. Your identity, strategy, and acquisition targets are never disclosed." ISBNdb notes that AI companies may not want to be caught destroying printed books during the scanning process. "The optics problem is real," ISBNdb's site says. "'AI company destroys two million books' is not a headline that generates sympathy."

sentences.en: An anonymous reader quotes a report from 404 Media: As AI companies search for more training data to improve their models, one company is offering old, printed books as an ideal source because they are guaranteed to be free of the very AI slop AI companies are producing.
sentences.zh: 一位匿名读者引用了 404 Media 的一篇报道：随着人工智能公司寻找更多的训练数据来改进他们的模型，一家公司正在提供旧的印刷书籍作为理想的来源，因为它们保证不含人工智能公司正在产生的人工智能废液。

sentences.en: "The world's best AI training data is sitting on a shelf," ISBNdb, a company that produces what it claims is "the world's largest book database," and that offers high-volume book acquisition services for AI companies, says on its site.
sentences.zh: ISBNdb 在其网站上表示：“世界上最好的人工智能训练数据就在书架上。”该公司生产自称“世界上最大的图书数据库”，并为人工智能公司提供大量图书获取服务。

sentences.en: "Books represent curated, peer-reviewed, domain-specific human knowledge, structured in a way no web crawl can replicate.
sentences.zh: “书籍代表了精心策划的、经过同行评审的、特定领域的人类知识，其结构是网络爬行无法复制的。

sentences.en: Dense, edited, authoritative."
sentences.zh: 密集、经过编辑、权威。”

sentences.en: In one article on its site, ISBNdb explains that printed books published before 2022 are ideal for AI training data because they don't include AI generated text.
sentences.zh: ISBNdb 在其网站上的一篇文章中解释说，2022 年之前出版的印刷书籍是人工智能训练数据的理想选择，因为它们不包含人工智能生成的文本。

sentences.en: As the article correctly notes, much of the data that AI companies can scrape from the internet today is likely to include AI generated text, which could result in "model collapse," a process by which AI models that are trained on AI generated data results in worse models that are more prone to errors.
sentences.zh: 正如文章正确指出的那样，人工智能公司今天可以从互联网上抓取的大部分数据可能包括人工智能生成的文本，这可能会导致“模型崩溃”，在这个过程中，接受人工智能生成数据训练的人工智能模型会产生更糟糕的模型，更容易出错。

sentences.en: The article also notes that book authors who object to their writing being scraped for training purposes can now easily poison AI models by producing writing designed to manipulate and sabotage the resulting AI models.
sentences.zh: 文章还指出，那些反对出于训练目的而抄袭其作品的书籍作者现在可以通过编写旨在操纵和破坏生成的人工智能模型的文章来轻松毒害人工智能模型。

sentences.en: "Print books from the pre-LLM era are structurally guaranteed to be free of this contamination.
sentences.zh: “法学硕士之前时代的印刷书籍在结构上保证不会受到这种污染。

sentences.en: That alone is a significant advantage [...]
sentences.zh: 仅此一点就是一个显着的优势[...]

sentences.en: "Physical books published before this date [pre-2022] are structurally clean of modern poisoning tools." [...]
sentences.zh: “在此日期之前（2022 年之前）出版的实体书在结构上没有现代中毒工具。” [...]

sentences.en: ISBNdb advertises that it can keep the identity of AI companies secret.
sentences.zh: ISBNdb 宣称它可以对人工智能公司的身份保密。

sentences.en: "Strict NDA [non-disclosure agreement] on every engagement," ISBNdb's site says.
sentences.zh: ISBNdb 网站称：“每次参与都遵守严格的 NDA（保密协议）”。

sentences.en: "Every project begins with a legally binding non-disclosure agreement.
sentences.zh: “每个项目都以具有法律约束力的保密协议开始。

sentences.en: Your identity, strategy, and acquisition targets are never disclosed."
sentences.zh: 你的身份、战略和收购目标永远不会被披露。”

sentences.en: ISBNdb notes that AI companies may not want to be caught destroying printed books during the scanning process.
sentences.zh: ISBNdb 指出，人工智能公司可能不希望被发现在扫描过程中破坏印刷书籍。

sentences.en: "The optics problem is real," ISBNdb's site says.
sentences.zh: ISBNdb 网站称：“光学问题确实存在。”

sentences.en: "'AI company destroys two million books' is not a headline that generates sympathy."
sentences.zh: “‘人工智能公司销毁了 200 万本书’并不是一个能引起同情的标题。”

sentences.zh: ISBNdb 网站称：“光学问题确实存在。”

content_html_zh: 一位匿名读者引用了 404 Media 的一篇报道：随着人工智能公司寻找更多的训练数据来改进他们的模型，一家公司正在提供旧的印刷书籍作为理想的来源，因为它们保证不含人工智能公司正在产生的人工智能废液。 ISBNdb 在其网站上表示：“世界上最好的人工智能训练数据就在书架上。”该公司生产自称“世界上最大的图书数据库”，并为人工智能公司提供大量图书获取服务。 “书籍代表了精心策划的、经过同行评审的、特定领域的人类知识，其结构是网络爬行无法复制的。 密集、经过编辑、权威。” ISBNdb 在其网站上的一篇文章中解释说，2022 年之前出版的印刷书籍是人工智能训练数据的理想选择，因为它们不包含人工智能生成的文本。 正如文章正确指出的那样，人工智能公司今天可以从互联网上抓取的大部分数据可能包括人工智能生成的文本，这可能会导致“模型崩溃”，在这个过程中，接受人工智能生成数据训练的人工智能模型会产生更糟糕的模型，更容易出错。 文章还指出，那些反对出于训练目的而抄袭其作品的书籍作者现在可以通过编写旨在操纵和破坏生成的人工智能模型的文章来轻松毒害人工智能模型。 “法学硕士之前时代的印刷书籍在结构上保证不会受到这种污染。 仅此一点就是一个显着的优势[...] “在此日期之前（2022 年之前）出版的实体书在结构上没有现代中毒工具。” [...] ISBNdb 宣称它可以对人工智能公司的身份保密。 ISBNdb 网站称：“每次参与都遵守严格的 NDA（保密协议）”。 “每个项目都以具有法律约束力的保密协议开始。 你的身份、战略和收购目标永远不会被披露。” ISBNdb 指出，人工智能公司可能不希望被发现在扫描过程中破坏印刷书籍。 ISBNdb 网站称：“光学问题确实存在。” “‘人工智能公司销毁了 200 万本书’并不是一个能引起同情的标题。”
